models.generation_context: 6th-gen Impreza, introduced 2024 as hatch-only (sedan discontinued); 2026 brings revised 2.5L engine for RS trim, latest EyeSight Driver Assist with Emergency Lane Keep Assist, and rear-seat side airbags as new standard safety equipment
models.model_summary: Compact all-wheel-drive 5-door hatchback. Standard Subaru Symmetrical AWD, Lineartronic CVT (no manual offered), and the latest EyeSight suite on both trims. Base trim (2.0L, 152 hp) is the entry-level Impreza Sport; the RS (2.5L, 180 hp) is the only step-up.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a VDS score for the 2026 Impreza (VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles). Consumer Reports rates the redesigned Impreza generation favorably for ride and value but does not display a public numeric predicted-reliability rating for 2026.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the 2026 Impreza.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the redesigned Impreza for its standard AWD, comfortable ride, comprehensive standard safety tech, and competitive pricing for an AWD compact, but consistently fault the 2.0L Sport for sluggish acceleration and the cabin for cost-conscious materials. The 2.5L RS is widely recommended as the volume pick.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the comfortable ride, roomy interior for its size, standard AWD and safety features, and good fuel economy; common complaints are road noise and modest power from the 2.0L base engine.

notable_other: Latest EyeSight with Emergency Lane Keep Assist
Rear-seat side airbags newly standard for 2026

trims.notes: 0-60 not published by Subaru; press coverage cites the 2.0L Sport as noticeably slower than RS but no published independent test for Sport. NHTSA has not yet rated the 2026 Impreza. IIHS 2026 awards (May 2026) did not include the Impreza on the TSP list. Subaru spec/feature pages render JS-only and returned thin content to WebFetch; dimensions and curb weight cross-checked against the 24MY Subaru media specs PDF (carryover platform).

notable_other: Leather-wrapped steering wheel and shift lever
Standard Blind-Spot Detection with Rear Cross-Traffic Alert
Red contrast stitching, sport seats with red bolsters

changes: Adds revised 2.5L BOXER engine (180 hp / 178 lb-ft vs Sport's 2.0L 152 hp / 145 lb-ft)
Adds standard heated front seats and heated steering wheel
Adds power driver's seat and wireless phone charging
Adds standard Blind-Spot Detection with Rear Cross-Traffic Alert
Adds leather-wrapped steering wheel, remote start, red interior accents

trims.notes: 0-60 of 7.8 sec from Car and Driver; Edmunds reported 8.4 sec in their independent test. Curb weight slightly higher than Sport due to additional equipment.

models.notes: Impreza is hatchback-only (sedan dropped after MY24). 2026 brings revised 2.5L BOXER for RS, latest-gen EyeSight with Emergency Lane Keep Assist, and standard rear-seat side airbags across both trims. subaru.com/vehicles/impreza pages render JS-only to WebFetch so dimensional data was cross-checked against Subaru media spec PDFs and Car and Driver/Edmunds; powertrain, pricing, transmission, and standard-equipment data sourced from the official Subaru media center press release. Tire spec 225/40R18 from carryover 24MY spec sheet; verify in build phase. Image entries all flag needs_scraping (subaru.com consumer pages return only nav/footer to WebFetch). Phase 4 image scrape: persistent low coverage (6.9% as of 2026-05-14) — Playwright surfaces 500+ candidates per page but Subaru's CDN naming uses feature labels not English angle words, so the angle-pattern matcher rarely fires. Same future fix as Hyundai. See reports/persistent_low_coverage_brands.md.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen Crosstrek introduced 2024; 2026 drops the 2.0L base engine and standardizes the 2.5L 180-hp BOXER across all gasoline trims, and adds an all-new next-generation series-parallel hybrid powertrain (1.1 kWh Li-ion battery, two electric motors, 194 hp combined) replacing the prior plug-in hybrid
models.model_summary: Compact AWD crossover based on the Impreza platform with raised ride height and rugged cladding. Two distinct powertrain lines for 2026: gasoline 2.5L 180-hp (Base, Premium, Sport, Limited, Wilderness) and new hybrid 194-hp HEV (Sport Hybrid, Limited Hybrid). Wilderness adds 9.3 in ground clearance, all-terrain tires, and a 3,500-lb towing rating.

reliability.summary: No public JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score for the 2026 Crosstrek or Crosstrek Hybrid. Outgoing Crosstrek generation has rated 'about average' historically; the Hybrid is brand-new for 2026 with no track record.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the 2026 Crosstrek.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the Crosstrek's standard AWD, off-pavement competence (especially Wilderness), and refreshed infotainment, while flagging modest gasoline acceleration and road noise. The new 194-hp Hybrid is widely described as the pick of the lineup: it solves the base car's pace problem and lifts combined MPG from 29 to 36, though at a price premium.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the all-weather capability, comfortable ride, and outward visibility; common complaints include sluggish acceleration (gasoline model) and dated infotainment behavior.

notable_other: Standard X-MODE with Hill Descent Control
Latest EyeSight with Emergency Lane Keep Assist

trims.notes: 2026 IIHS rating: Good on side and headlights, Marginal on moderate overlap front (rear passenger lap belt issue) — no TSP award. NHTSA has not yet rated the 2026 Crosstrek. Towing 1,500 lb on non-Wilderness trims. Curb weight from Subaru spec/dealer mirrors; verify in build phase.

notable_other: Upgrades from 7-inch to 11.6-inch Multimedia Plus touchscreen

trims.notes: Same powertrain, fuel economy, dimensions, safety, and warranty as Base.

notable_other: Blind-Spot Detection with Rear Cross-Traffic Alert standard

changes: Adds dual-mode X-MODE with extra off-road traction modes
Adds standard Blind-Spot Detection with Rear Cross-Traffic Alert
Adds hands-free power rear gate and remote start

trims.notes: Same 2.5L powertrain and EPA MPG as Base.

notable_other: Heated steering wheel and rear outboard seats

changes: Adds heated steering wheel and heated rear outboard seats
Adds wireless phone charging and built-in navigation
Adds standard Blind-Spot Detection with Rear Cross-Traffic Alert

trims.notes: Same 2.5L powertrain and EPA MPG as Base.

notable_other: 3,500-lb towing capacity (vs 1,500 lb other gas trims)
All-terrain Yokohama GEOLANDAR tires on matte-black 17-inch alloys
Exclusive front/rear bumpers with hexagonal grille
StarTex water-repellent upholstery with copper-tone stitching
Dual-mode X-MODE with Snow/Dirt and Deep Snow/Mud modes

trims.notes: EPA dropped 3 MPG city/4 highway vs standard Crosstrek due to taller tires and revised final drive.

notable_other: Next-generation series-parallel hybrid (1.1 kWh Li-ion, dual motors, 194 hp combined)
Standard power moonroof, hands-free power liftgate

trims.notes: Base trim of the Crosstrek Hybrid powertrain line (atomic: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). HEV — not a plug-in. Manufacturer-combined system hp is 194 with no separately published combined torque. Total range 597 mi from EPA listing. Cargo slightly reduced vs gas (battery under cargo floor). Curb weight ~348 lb heavier than gas Base.

notable_other: Leather-trimmed seating with orange contrast stitching
Heated steering wheel and rear outboard seats

changes: Adds heated steering wheel and heated rear outboard seats

trims.notes: Same hybrid powertrain, fuel economy (36/36/36), and dimensions as Sport Hybrid.

models.notes: Crosstrek has two powertrain lines for 2026: gasoline 2.5L (Base, Premium, Sport, Limited, Wilderness) and all-new HEV Hybrid (Sport Hybrid, Limited Hybrid). Per spec §6.4 each powertrain line has its own base trim — Base for gas line, Sport Hybrid for hybrid line, both with is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null. 2026 drops the previous 2.0L base engine (the entire gas lineup now uses the 2.5L 180-hp BOXER) and replaces the previous Toyota-co-developed PHEV with an all-new in-house series-parallel hybrid. Hybrid wears unique 18-inch alloys and slightly smaller cargo hold due to under-floor battery. IIHS testing returned Marginal on moderate overlap front (rear passenger lap belt issue) — no TSP for 2026. Phase 4 image scrape: persistent low coverage (6.9% as of 2026-05-14) — Playwright surfaces 500+ candidates per page but Subaru's CDN naming uses feature labels not English angle words, so the angle-pattern matcher rarely fires. Same future fix as Hyundai. See reports/persistent_low_coverage_brands.md.

models.generation_context: 6th-gen Forester introduced 2025; 2026 adds all-new Hybrid powertrain (194 hp combined, 1.1 kWh Li-ion, series-parallel HEV) and Sport Onyx Edition; refreshed pricing in Feb 2026; new IIHS Top Safety Pick+ award
models.model_summary: Subaru's volume compact AWD SUV with two powertrain lines for 2026: 2.5L 180-hp BOXER ICE (Base, Premium, Sport, Sport Onyx, Limited, Touring, Wilderness) and a new 194-hp HEV Hybrid (Premium, Sport, Limited, Touring). Wilderness is gas-only with 9.3 in ground clearance and 3,500-lb towing. 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ award applies to both gas and hybrid.

reliability.summary: No public JD Power VDS score for the 2026 Forester (current-gen too new for VDS, which evaluates 3-year-old vehicles). Consumer Reports has not yet published a 2026 predicted-reliability score; prior-gen Forester rated 'about average'. The new hybrid powertrain has no track record.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the 2026 Forester.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the redesigned Forester for its expanded cargo, comprehensive standard EyeSight suite, refined ride, and brand-typical AWD all-weather competence, with the new Hybrid singled out for transforming the driving experience (40% better city MPG and noticeably stronger acceleration). Common critiques are uninspired styling and middling on-road dynamics versus newer rivals.

owner_reviews.summary: 2026 Forester is the second model year of the new generation; consumer reviews exist on Edmunds/KBB but published numeric aggregates were not extractable to WebFetch. Owners generally praise visibility, safety tech, and AWD; common complaints are road noise and modest power.

notable_other: Latest EyeSight with Emergency Lane Keep Assist and Automatic Emergency Steering
Larger cargo area than other Forester trims (no sunroof intrusion)

trims.notes: Base Forester has no panoramic moonroof, so cargo volume (29.6 / 74.4 cu ft) is greater than other gas trims (27.5 / 69.1 cu ft). 2026 IIHS TSP+ award. NHTSA has not yet rated 2026 Forester.

notable_other: Upgrades to 11.6-inch Multimedia Plus touchscreen with wireless CarPlay/Android Auto
Adds heated front seats and heated side mirrors
Adds 10-way power driver's seat with lumbar

Adds 10-way power driver's seat, hands-free power rear gate, wireless phone charging, remote start

trims.notes: Same 2.5L 180-hp powertrain and 26/33/29 MPG as Base. Cargo drops 2.1/5.3 cu ft due to panoramic moonroof intrusion.

notable_other: Gray water-repellent StarTex with copper-tone stitching
Standard Blind-Spot Detection with Rear Cross-Traffic Alert

11.6-inch Multimedia Plus, panoramic moonroof, hands-free power liftgate

Curb weight estimated +280 lb vs gas Premium due to hybrid hardware. IIHS Top Safety Pick+ award applies to hybrid trims as well.

notable_other: Ventilated front seats, heated rear outboard seats

trims.notes: Flagship hybrid trim. Same hybrid powertrain and MPG as Premium Hybrid.

models.notes: Forester has 2 powertrain lines for 2026: gas 2.5L (Base, Premium, Sport, Sport Onyx, Limited, Touring, Wilderness) and brand-new HEV Hybrid (Premium Hybrid, Sport Hybrid, Limited Hybrid, Touring Hybrid). Each powertrain line has its own base trim (Base for gas, Premium Hybrid for hybrid) per spec §6.4. Wilderness is gas-only. Pricing reflects Feb 2026 revised MSRPs (subaru.com press release #2421). Both gas and hybrid earn 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+. Sport Onyx Edition is new for 2026, slotting between Sport and Limited. Phase 4 image scrape: persistent low coverage (6.9% as of 2026-05-14) — Playwright surfaces 500+ candidates per page but Subaru's CDN naming uses feature labels not English angle words, so the angle-pattern matcher rarely fires. Same future fix as Hyundai. See reports/persistent_low_coverage_brands.md.

models.generation_context: 7th-gen Outback all-new for 2026 — taller, boxier body style aligning it with two-row midsize SUV competitors (Honda Passport, Toyota 4Runner). Sedan/wagon styling cues replaced by upright SUV silhouette. Wilderness arrives early 2026 with redesigned styling.
models.model_summary: Subaru's midsize AWD crossover, reimagined for 2026 with taller, more upright SUV proportions. Two engines: 2.5L 180-hp NA BOXER (Premium, Limited, Touring) and 2.4L 260-hp turbo BOXER (Limited XT, Touring XT, Wilderness). Standard 12.1-inch touchscreen and 12.3-inch digital cluster across the line. Wilderness adds 9.5 in ground clearance and 3,500-lb tow rating. IIHS Top Safety Pick+ award for 2026.

reliability.summary: No public JD Power VDS score for the 2026 Outback — first model year of all-new generation with no track record. Consumer Reports has not yet published a numeric predicted-reliability score for the redesigned 2026.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the 2026 Outback.

professional_reviews.summary: Press coverage of the all-new 2026 Outback praises the more practical SUV body, generous cargo and upright seating position, expanded standard tech (12.1-inch screen, 12.3-inch cluster), and rugged Wilderness variant; the redesign sacrifices the wagon-style silhouette enthusiasts loved and adds curb weight. The 260-hp turbo XT is universally preferred over the underpowered 180-hp NA.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient owner-review volume on Edmunds/KBB for the all-new 2026 Outback at time of research. Prior-gen owners broadly praised AWD capability, comfort, and Subaru's safety reputation; complaints focused on infotainment software and CVT engine drone.

notable_other: Heated front seats and 10-way power driver's seat

trims.notes: Base trim of the Outback ICE line. Towing 2,700 lb on NA 2.5L trims (3,500 lb on 2.4T trims per Subaru marketing). 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ award covers Outback. NHTSA has not yet rated 2026 Outback.

notable_other: Heated steering wheel and heated rear outboard seats
Blind-Spot Detection with Rear Cross-Traffic Alert

trims.notes: Standalone trim family. Wilderness gets electronic dampers — a first for any Wilderness trim. Arrived at dealers Q1 2026, slightly after the rest of the lineup.

models.notes: All-new 7th-gen Outback for 2026: taller, boxier SUV-style proportions. 6 trims across one model with two engines (180-hp 2.5L NA on Premium/Limited/Touring, 260-hp 2.4T on Limited XT/Touring XT/Wilderness). Same ICE powertrain type so all are one powertrain line with Premium as base. Wilderness now uses electronic dampers and is gas-turbo-only. 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ applies to Outback. All MSRPs from official Subaru media press release. Dimensions cited from Subaru.com (and dealer mirrors) with Wilderness 9.5-in clearance and 191.7-in length verified across multiple sources. Phase 4 image scrape: persistent low coverage (6.9% as of 2026-05-14) — Playwright surfaces 500+ candidates per page but Subaru's CDN naming uses feature labels not English angle words, so the angle-pattern matcher rarely fires. Same future fix as Hyundai. See reports/persistent_low_coverage_brands.md.

models.generation_context: 2nd-gen Ascent introduced 2024 refresh; 2026 carries that styling with revised pricing, Limited Bronze Edition and Onyx Edition Touring sub-variants
models.model_summary: Subaru's 3-row family SUV — 7 or 8 seats, standard 260-hp turbo 2.4L BOXER, Symmetrical AWD, 5,000-lb tow rating. 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ award. Premium Bronze and Onyx editions add styling treatments to mid- and top-trim levels.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not yet published a 2026 Ascent VDS score. Consumer Reports has historically rated the Ascent below average for predicted reliability in earlier years; 2024+ refresh data not yet aggregated.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the 2026 Ascent.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Ascent's family-friendly interior, standard 5,000-lb towing, comprehensive standard EyeSight safety suite, and the smooth turbo BOXER, while consistently noting that the platform is starting to show its age versus newer rivals like the Toyota Grand Highlander, Mazda CX-90, and Kia Telluride.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the Ascent's interior space, AWD all-weather capability, and standard EyeSight; common complaints are CVT engine drone under load and dated infotainment.

notable_other: 8-passenger 2nd-row bench standard (7-pass captain's chairs no-cost option)
11.6-inch Multimedia Plus touchscreen with wireless CarPlay/Android Auto
Standard Blind-Spot Detection with Rear Cross-Traffic Alert

trims.notes: Base trim. Premium uses fueleconomy.gov ID 50021 (19/26/22); Limited and higher trims drop slightly to 19/25/21 (ID 50022) due to larger 20-inch wheels.

notable_other: Heated steering wheel and heated rear outboard seats
Surround View Monitor with built-in navigation
Power liftgate with memory and hands-free open

models.notes: All 5 Ascent trims share the 260-hp 2.4L turbo BOXER (one powertrain line). Limited Bronze Edition and Onyx Edition Touring are singleton trim_families (sole-trim styling editions). EPA splits Ascent into two entries (50021 = Premium with 18-inch wheels = 19/26/22; 50022 = all higher trims with 20-inch wheels = 19/25/21). 2026 IIHS Top Safety Pick+ award applies to Ascent. NHTSA has not yet rated 2026 Ascent. Phase 4 image scrape: persistent low coverage (6.9% as of 2026-05-14) — Playwright surfaces 500+ candidates per page but Subaru's CDN naming uses feature labels not English angle words, so the angle-pattern matcher rarely fires. Same future fix as Hyundai. See reports/persistent_low_coverage_brands.md.

models.generation_context: 2nd-gen BRZ (ZD8) introduced 2022; 2026 drops the entry-level Premium trim, leaving Limited as the new base. tS returns with STI-tuned Hitachi dampers and Brembos; new limited-run Series.Yellow (350 units) tops the lineup
models.model_summary: Subaru's RWD 2+2 sports coupe (co-developed with the Toyota GR86). 228 hp 2.4L flat-four BOXER, low center of gravity (17.95 in), 6-speed manual standard with available automatic on Limited only. tS adds STI-tuned dampers and Brembo brakes; Series.Yellow is a 350-unit Sunrise Yellow special based on tS.

reliability.summary: No model-specific 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted-reliability score for the BRZ (low-volume sports coupe). Brand-level Subaru scored 181 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — well below the 204 PP100 industry average, placing Subaru 6th overall among all brands and top-tier mass-market. Subaru also ranked #2 in the 2026 Consumer Reports Annual Auto Reliability Survey. The Toyota-shared GR86/BRZ chassis and Subaru FA24 BOXER have shown solid early-build reliability per industry coverage.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the BRZ (low US volume).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers universally praise the BRZ's chassis balance, light weight, and engaging RWD manual driving experience at an accessible price; the tS upgrade with Brembos and STI-tuned dampers is widely considered the pick. Common critiques are modest naturally aspirated power, basic cabin tech, and noise on long highway drives.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners universally praise the BRZ for handling, manual shifter feel, and value; common complaints are modest straight-line power, road noise on long highway drives, and lack of cargo space.

notable_other: Ultrasuede upholstery with red leather bolsters
Latest EyeSight Driver Assist Technology (automatic transmission only — note manual offers fewer ADAS features)
Aluminum hood for low center of gravity

trims.notes: BRZ is RWD-only — the only non-AWD Subaru in the US lineup. Manual transmission BRZ does NOT include EyeSight ADAS (Subaru only fits EyeSight to the automatic). Automatic adds adaptive cruise, pre-collision braking, lane-departure assist, blind-spot detection, rear cross-traffic. EPA 20/27/22 for manual (ID 49787); automatic is 21/30/25 (ID 49788). Manual quoted as base entry. NHTSA has not rated; IIHS has not awarded the BRZ TSP.

notable_other: STI-tuned Hitachi performance dampers (front and rear)
Brembo braking system: gold 4-piston front / 2-piston rear calipers, larger rotors and pads
Recaro front sport seats with blue Ultrasuede inserts
tS-exclusive blue interior stitching and badges

trims.notes: Same EPA as manual Limited (20/27/22). Sole trim in 'brz-ts' family — singleton, so all 4 image angles required directly on this trim. Per spec, BRZ tS is treated as a trim within BRZ (not separate model) since Subaru markets it on the BRZ model page rather than its own showroom page.

notable_other: Production limited to 350 units in US
Black Ultrasuede upholstery with leather bolsters and yellow contrast stitching
All tS performance content (Hitachi dampers, Brembo brakes)

trims.notes: 350-unit limited-production special edition. Manual only. Singleton trim family. Performance equipment identical to tS.

models.notes: BRZ is the only RWD Subaru. EPA splits between manual (49787 = 20/27/22, premium fuel) and automatic (49788 = 21/30/25, premium fuel). Automatic is only available on Limited per Subaru press release. BRZ tS and Series.Yellow are MANUAL-ONLY. tS and Series.Yellow are trim-within-BRZ per Subaru marketing — not separate models. tS and Series.Yellow are each sole-trim singleton families requiring all 4 image angles on each. Premium trim was dropped for 2026, making Limited the new base trim. NHTSA has not rated BRZ; IIHS has not awarded TSP. Phase 4 image scrape: persistent low coverage (6.9% as of 2026-05-14) — Playwright surfaces 500+ candidates per page but Subaru's CDN naming uses feature labels not English angle words, so the angle-pattern matcher rarely fires. Same future fix as Hyundai. See reports/persistent_low_coverage_brands.md.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen WRX (VB) introduced 2022; 2026 brings back the entry Base trim (dropped in MY25) at $32,495, adds new tS-based Series.Yellow limited edition, and applies significant price reductions across the lineup
models.model_summary: Subaru's rally-inspired turbocharged AWD sports sedan. 271-hp 2.4L turbo BOXER, standard 6-speed manual on most trims (Subaru Performance Transmission CVT-based auto available on Limited and standard on GT), Symmetrical AWD. tS adds STI-tuned electronic dampers and Brembo brakes; Series.Yellow is the 2026 limited-run Sunrise Yellow tS-based special.

reliability.summary: No model-specific 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports predicted-reliability score for the WRX (low-volume performance sedan). Brand-level Subaru scored 181 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — well below the 204 PP100 industry average. Subaru also ranked #2 in the 2026 Consumer Reports Annual Auto Reliability Survey. The 5th-gen WRX (VB) FA24 turbo BOXER has not shown systemic concerns in the current generation, though performance owners typically run consumables (tires, brakes, clutches) harder.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the WRX (lower US volume).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the WRX's standard AWD turbo BOXER, manual gearbox, sharper steering and chassis tuning, and the tS's STI-developed electronic dampers and Brembos; common complaints are dated interior plastics versus rivals and unrefined engine sounds. Significant 2026 price cuts make it newly competitive against rivals like the Honda Civic Type R and Hyundai Elantra N.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the WRX's turbo punch, standard AWD all-weather capability, and direct manual gearbox; common complaints are road noise, premium-fuel cost, and middling interior materials.

notable_other: EyeSight with DriverFocus distraction mitigation (standard with manual transmission for 2026)
18-inch alloy wheels with summer performance tires

trims.notes: Returns for 2026 as the entry trim ($32,495) — previously dropped in MY25. EPA splits manual (50066 = 19/26/22) and automatic SPT (50067 = 18/25/21). Curb weight ~3,351 lb carryover from 2025. NHTSA has not rated; IIHS has not awarded WRX TSP for 2026.

Adds keyless access with push-button start

trims.notes: Singleton trim family. Limited-edition based on tS. Manual only.

models.notes: WRX returns the Base trim for 2026 (lowest-priced WRX since the prior generation), with significant price cuts versus MY25. EPA splits manual (50066 = 19/26/22) and SPT auto (50067 = 18/25/21). Manual is standard on Base/Premium/Limited/tS/Series.Yellow; SPT (CVT-based 8-speed) is optional on Limited and standard on GT. GT, tS, and Series.Yellow are singleton trim families. tS and Series.Yellow share STI-tuned dampers and Brembos. NHTSA has not yet rated 2026 WRX. IIHS has not awarded WRX TSP. Phase 4 image scrape: persistent low coverage (6.9% as of 2026-05-14) — Playwright surfaces 500+ candidates per page but Subaru's CDN naming uses feature labels not English angle words, so the angle-pattern matcher rarely fires. Same future fix as Hyundai. See reports/persistent_low_coverage_brands.md.

models.generation_context: Refreshed Solterra (Subaru's first dedicated BEV, co-developed with Toyota bZ4X) for 2026. Updated styling, new illuminated star-cluster fascia, larger 74.7-kWh battery, native NACS charging port, new high-performance Solterra XT variant with 338 hp combined and sub-5-second 0-60 mph
models.model_summary: Subaru's all-electric AWD compact SUV. Two motor configurations for 2026: 233-hp dual-motor (Premium, Limited) and new 338-hp dual-motor XT (Limited XT, Touring XT). 74.7-kWh battery, 288-mile EPA range (Premium with 18-inch wheels) or 278 miles (20-inch wheels and XT trims). Native NACS port unlocks 15,000+ Tesla Supercharger stations. DC fast charging 10-80% in approximately 30 minutes at 150 kW.

reliability.summary: No JD Power VDS score for the 2026 Solterra (BEVs in their first or second model year are rarely covered). Consumer Reports' road test rates the refreshed Solterra positively. The Solterra/bZ4X platform had documented early-production issues (wheel-bearing recalls) on MY22-23 that have since been addressed.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the 2026 Solterra.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the refreshed Solterra finally catches up to mainstream EV competitors: faster charging, larger battery, NACS standard, and a much more powerful XT variant. Highlights include standard AWD, refined ride, and Subaru's safety reputation; common critiques are slightly slower DC charging speed (150 kW vs rivals' 200+ kW) and that the base Premium's 233-hp motors still feel tepid versus newer EV SUV competition.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient 2026 owner-review volume; carryover from prior years shows owners praise the standard AWD and Subaru safety tech, with common complaints centered on charging speed (now addressed) and modest range (now addressed).

notable_other: Native NACS port (15,000+ Tesla Supercharger access)
150 kW DC fast charging, 10-80% in ~30 min
EyeSight Driver Assist with Blind-Spot Detection and Rear Cross-Traffic Alert
Restyled exterior with illuminated star-cluster fascia

trims.notes: Base trim of the Solterra (single powertrain line — all trims are EVs, but the XT variants have a different higher-power motor pair). 0-60 of ~6.5 sec is a Subaru-published estimate. Premium uses 18-inch aluminum wheels with aero covers and earns the 288-mile EPA rating (ID 49982). NHTSA has not rated. IIHS has not awarded TSP.

trims.notes: Flagship Solterra trim. Same 338-hp powertrain as Limited XT. Same EPA as 20-inch trims. EV warranty is 8yr/100k per Subaru EV policy.

models.notes: Solterra is the BEV powertrain line — single powertrain type but two motor configurations (Premium/Limited at 233 hp; Limited XT/Touring XT at 338 hp). Treating all as one BEV powertrain line with Premium as base per spec §6.4 (the XT motor pair is the same powertrain TYPE, just higher output — analogous to NA vs turbo within Outback). EPA splits 18-inch (49982 = 288 mi, MPGe 131/109/120) vs 20-inch (49981 = 278 mi, MPGe 128/105/117). Native NACS port + Tesla Supercharger access is new for 2026. NHTSA has not rated; IIHS has not awarded TSP. Per spec §3.6 EV trims mirror MPGe into fuel_economy.city/highway/combined. Phase 4 image scrape: persistent low coverage (6.9% as of 2026-05-14) — Playwright surfaces 500+ candidates per page but Subaru's CDN naming uses feature labels not English angle words, so the angle-pattern matcher rarely fires. Same future fix as Hyundai. See reports/persistent_low_coverage_brands.md.

models.generation_context: All-new 2026 Subaru BEV SUV — Subaru's second dedicated EV. Sized between Solterra (compact) and Outback (midsize); 6 inches longer and ~1 inch taller than Solterra. Dual-motor 375 hp standard, native NACS port, 3,500-lb tow rating, 8.5 in ground clearance
models.model_summary: All-new 2026 mid-size AWD electric SUV slotting between Solterra and Outback. Standard dual-motor 375-hp AWD-electric powertrain, 74.7-kWh battery, EPA range up to 281 miles (Premium with 18-inch wheels), 3,500-lb towing, 8.5-inch ground clearance, native NACS port, 4.4-sec 0-60 mph. Three trims: Premium, Limited, Touring. Designed for adventure-oriented buyers who want EV capability with Outback-style ruggedness.

reliability.summary: All-new 2026 EV; no model-specific reliability track record. Brand-level Subaru scored 181 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — well below the 204 PP100 industry average, and #2 in the 2026 Consumer Reports Annual Auto Reliability Survey. Trailseeker is built on a Toyota-Subaru shared BEV platform (related to bZ4X/Solterra), which has documented early-production issues addressed via recalls. Early-year reliability for the new platform variant remains unknown.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the all-new Trailseeker.

professional_reviews.summary: Early press coverage of the all-new Trailseeker highlights the 375-hp dual-motor standard powertrain, 3,500-lb tow rating, 8.5-inch ground clearance with proper X-MODE off-road modes, and native NACS port; it brings credible Subaru AWD adventure DNA to the BEV space. Cargo space exceeds Solterra. Some reviewers note real-world DC fast charging tops at 150 kW which trails newer 800V EV competitors.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient owner-review data — Trailseeker launched in Q1 2026.

notable_other: Native NACS port (15,000+ Tesla Supercharger access)
150 kW DC fast charge, 10-80% in ~28 min
X-MODE with Snow/Dirt and Deep Snow/Mud modes
14-inch infotainment, dual wireless charging pads, 4 USB-C ports

trims.notes: Base trim of the sole Trailseeker powertrain line (all trims are 375-hp dual-motor BEVs). Premium uses 18-inch wheels (EPA 281 mi / 117 MPGe combined, ID 50300). Curb weight is an estimate — Subaru has not yet published exact curb weight. NHTSA has not rated; IIHS has not awarded TSP for this all-new model.

models.notes: All-new 2026 Subaru BEV SUV. Premium = base ($39,995 + $1,450 D&D = $41,445), Limited = $43,995 (some early sources quote $45,445 with destination as the 'Limited' price — KBB lists Touring at $46,555 with destination; subtract $1,450 = $45,105 base MSRP for Touring). Subaru's press release headline price is $39,995 starting. EPA splits 18-inch (50300 = 281 mi, MPGe 126/107/117) vs 20-inch (50299 = 274 mi, MPGe 125/103/114). All three trims share the 375-hp dual-motor AWD powertrain. NHTSA has not rated; IIHS has not awarded TSP. Per spec §3.6 EV trims mirror MPGe into fuel_economy.city/highway/combined. Phase 4 image scrape: persistent low coverage (6.9% as of 2026-05-14) — Playwright surfaces 500+ candidates per page but Subaru's CDN naming uses feature labels not English angle words, so the angle-pattern matcher rarely fires. Same future fix as Hyundai. See reports/persistent_low_coverage_brands.md.

models.generation_context: All-new 2026 Subaru BEV — Subaru's third EV in three model years (Solterra, Trailseeker, Uncharted). Subcompact crossover-coupe styling, FWD entry available (a first for Subaru's modern EV lineup), assembled in Japan, native NACS port
models.model_summary: Subaru's new subcompact crossover-coupe BEV — lowest-priced Subaru EV at $34,995. Two motor configurations: single-motor 221-hp FWD (Premium, the only FWD Subaru in the 2026 lineup) and dual-motor 338-hp AWD (Sport, GT). 74.7-kWh battery, EPA range up to 308 miles (Premium FWD), native NACS port for Tesla Supercharger access, 150-kW DC fast charging.

reliability.summary: All-new 2026 BEV; no model-specific reliability track record. Brand-level Subaru scored 181 PP100 in the 2026 JD Power VDS — well below the 204 PP100 industry average, and #2 in the 2026 Consumer Reports Annual Auto Reliability Survey. Uncharted is Subaru's third EV in three model years; sourced from a Toyota partnership and assembled in Japan. Early-year reliability for this new model is unknown.

customer_satisfaction.summary: J.D. Power has not yet released the 2026 U.S. APEAL Study (typically published in July). No model-specific APEAL score has been published for the all-new Uncharted.

professional_reviews.summary: Early press coverage describes the Uncharted as Subaru's most accessible EV (entry below $35K), with a true 300+ mile EPA range, native NACS port, and 338-hp Sport/GT dual-motor performance variants. The FWD-only base trim and Subaru's coupe-SUV styling represent unusual choices for the brand; reviewers will be watching how it competes against the Hyundai Kona Electric, Chevy Equinox EV, and Volvo EX30.

owner_reviews.summary: Insufficient owner-review data — Uncharted launched in early 2026.

notable_other: FWD-only — the only FWD trim in the 2026 Subaru US lineup
74.7 kWh battery, EPA 308 mi (highest range in the 2026 Subaru lineup)
Native NACS port for Tesla Supercharger access
150 kW DC fast charging, 10-80% in ~28 min

trims.notes: Base trim of the Uncharted FWD powertrain line. Atomic: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null (sole trim in FWD powertrain line). FWD with 221-hp single motor — the only non-AWD Subaru in the 2026 lineup besides BRZ. EPA 50301 (308 mi, 140/118/129 MPGe). 0-60 not published by Subaru; estimated ~7.5-8 sec by reviewers. Dimensions estimated from press release context (6 inches shorter than Solterra) and KBB; verify in build phase. NHTSA has not rated; IIHS has not awarded TSP for this all-new model.

notable_other: Standard X-MODE dual-mode (Snow/Dirt and Deep Snow/Mud)
All Premium FWD content (14-inch infotainment, NACS, 11 kW AC)

trims.notes: Base trim of the Uncharted AWD-electric (338 hp) powertrain line per spec §6.4 multi-powertrain rule (the 338-hp AWD dual-motor is a fundamentally different drivetrain from the 221-hp FWD single-motor). Atomic: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null. EPA 50303 (287 mi, 127/106/117 MPGe). Sport adds dual-motor AWD + X-MODE versus Premium FWD.

notable_other: Upgrades to 20-inch alloys (drops range to 273 mi)
Adds ventilated front seats and memory driver's seat

changes: Upgrades to 20-inch alloys (drops EPA range to 273 mi, MPGe 117 → 112)
Adds ventilated front seats and memory driver's seat

trims.notes: Same 338-hp dual-motor AWD powertrain as Sport. EPA 50302 (273 mi, 123/101/112 MPGe). Wheelbase + dimensions inherited from Sport.

models.notes: Uncharted is Subaru's third 2026 BEV — assembled in Japan (unlike Solterra/Trailseeker also Japan-built). The trim line has TWO powertrain lines per spec §6.4: (1) FWD 221-hp single-motor (Premium FWD only — sole trim, is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null atomic), and (2) AWD-electric 338-hp dual-motor (Sport = base, GT = step-up). Both base trims (Premium FWD and Sport) carry is_base_trim true with delta_from_base null per the multi-powertrain rule. EPA: Premium FWD = 50301 (308 mi, FWD); Sport AWD (18-inch) = 50303 (287 mi); GT AWD (20-inch) = 50302 (273 mi). NACS native across all trims. NHTSA has not rated; IIHS has not awarded TSP for this all-new model. Some dimensional values (curb weight, exact length/width/height/wheelbase/cargo) are estimated from the press release's comparison to Solterra (Uncharted is described as smaller — 6 inches shorter than Solterra) plus KBB/Edmunds early data; verify in build phase. Phase 4 image scrape: persistent low coverage (6.9% as of 2026-05-14) — Playwright surfaces 500+ candidates per page but Subaru's CDN naming uses feature labels not English angle words, so the angle-pattern matcher rarely fires. Same future fix as Hyundai. See reports/persistent_low_coverage_brands.md.
